solution: 
```java
class Solution {
    public String maximumBinaryString(String binary) {
        int n = binary.length();
        int zeroCount = 0;
        int firstZeroIndex = -1;

        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++) {
            if (binary.charAt(i) == '0') {
                zeroCount++;
                if (firstZeroIndex == -1) {
                    firstZeroIndex = i;
                }
            }
        }

        if (zeroCount <= 1) {
            return binary;
        }

        char[] result = binary.toCharArray();
        for (int i = firstZeroIndex; i < firstZeroIndex + zeroCount - 1; i++) {
            result[i] = '1';
        }
        result[firstZeroIndex + zeroCount - 1] = '0';

        for (int i = firstZeroIndex + zeroCount; i < n; i++) {
            if (result[i] == '0') {
                result[i] = '1';
            }
        }

        return new String(result);
    }
}
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n)
space complexity: O(n)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: The solution identifies the first occurrence of '0' and the total number of '0's. It then transforms the sequence of '0's into a single '0' followed by '1's, maximizing the binary string's value. This approach is efficient and leverages the allowed operations to optimize the result.
